# Aetas in the Workplace — Charities & Not-for-Profits

> A specialist financial wellbeing and governance service for charities and not-for-profit organisations. Delivered through a partnership between Aetas in the Workplace and Adena Street Limited.

Aetas in the Workplace (Aetas ITW) provides structured support to charities and not-for-profit organisations across three service areas: governance, financial guidance, and staff financial wellbeing. The service is built for organisations with between 10 and 200 employees and is designed to be bespoke to each organisation rather than template-driven.

The governance and financial guidance services are provided independently by Adena Street Limited. The financial wellbeing service is delivered by Aetas in the Workplace. Where regulated financial advice is required, this is provided by Aetas Wealth, a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (registration number 458421).

## Pages

- [Home — Charities Overview](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/index.html): Overview of the full three-service offering for charities and not-for-profits. Covers the challenge facing the sector, why Aetas ITW is different from corporate-designed solutions, the income opportunity angle (legacy giving, corporate donations), and how to get started.

- [Governance](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/governance.html): Detailed page on the governance service offered by Adena Street. Covers Investment Policy Statements (IPS), trustee training, board effectiveness, ethical investment frameworks, conflict of interest management, and Charity Commission compliance.

- [Financial Guidance](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/financial-guidance.html): Detailed page on the financial guidance service offered by Adena Street. Covers investment performance benchmarking (including ARC Charity Indices), fee transparency and OCF analysis, cash management strategy, and investment manager search and review.

- [Financial Wellbeing](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/financial-wellbeing.html): Detailed page on the staff financial wellbeing service powered by Aetas in the Workplace. Covers employee benefits consultancy, financial resilience workshops, confidential one-to-one guidance from a regulated financial planner, and wellbeing initiatives through the Aetas Collective.

- [FAQs](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/faqs.html): Frequently asked questions organised by service area — Getting Started, Governance, Financial Guidance, and Financial Wellbeing. Covers affordability, IPS requirements, OCF charges, EAPs, one-to-one guidance, and the discovery conversation process.

- [Insights](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/insights.html): Thought leadership articles on governance, financial guidance, financial wellbeing, and income strategy for charities. Topics include IPS reviews, hidden investment fees (OCF), Charity Commission expectations (CC14), staff retention in the not-for-profit sector, legacy giving, and cash management.

- [Diagnostic](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/diagnostic.html): A 12-question self-assessment across the three service areas. Produces a scored result (out of 8 per area) with tailored interpretation and links to relevant service pages.

- [Book a Meeting](https://charities.aetaspartners.com/book-a-meeting.html): Discovery conversation booking page with embedded GoHighLevel calendar. Includes context on what the conversation covers, what to have to hand, and direct contact details for Matthew Steiner.

## Key Concepts

**Investment Policy Statement (IPS):** A formal document setting out a charity's investment objectives, risk appetite, ethical restrictions, and governance framework. A legal requirement for any charity that has delegated discretionary investment management to an external manager. Should be reviewed annually.

**OCF (Ongoing Charges Figure):** A fee embedded within investment funds, deducted directly from fund assets rather than charged as a visible line item. Frequently undisclosed in trustee reporting and can significantly erode net returns.

**ARC Charity Indices:** Sterling-denominated, risk-based benchmarks used to assess charity investment performance against peer organisations. A standard tool for independent performance benchmarking.

**CC14:** Charity Commission guidance on investing charity money. Sets out trustee duties around investment policy, manager oversight, ethical restrictions, and documentation.

**Financial wellbeing vs financial advice:** Workshops and group sessions delivered by Aetas ITW are educational and do not constitute regulated financial advice. Where regulated advice is required, this is provided separately by Aetas Wealth (FCA registration 458421).
